articles.title: As Trump and Xi Meet in China, American and Chinese People Are Drifting Apart
articles.summary: Past meetings between the presidents of the U.S. and China have involved friendly displays of cultural exchange. Those gestures have largely dried up.

articles.summary: President Xi Jinping of China is buying time for Beijing and may see an opening with a U.S. president weakened by the war in Iran.

articles.title: What's at stake for the people of Thitu Island as U.S. and Chinese leaders meet
articles.summary: As U.S. and Chinese leaders meet this week, the world is watching, and so are residents of Thitu Island in the disputed South China Sea, known in the Philippines as the West Philippine Sea.

articles.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
articles.summary: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

articles.title: Justice Dept. Sues D.C. Bar Over Efforts to Discipline Government Lawyers

articles.title: Israel Says Netanyahu Made a Secret Trip to U.A.E., Which Says He Didn’t
articles.summary: The claim and the denial signaled both how close Israel and the United Arab Emirates have become, and how delicate that relationship remains.

articles.title: A severe mouse plague puts Australian crops in danger
articles.summary: Australia's grain farmers, already strained by war-driven shortages, now face a severe mouse plague that could devastate crops in a key growing region.

articles.summary: The U.S. and Iran remain deadlocked in the Strait of Hormuz, with no clear way forward.

articles.title: Five countries boycott Eurovision Song Contest over Israel's participation

articles.summary: The president’s top military adviser is walking a tightrope as he leads the military through a divisive and unpopular war.

articles.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve

articles.title: Neanderthals may have drilled out a cavity 59,000 years ago
articles.summary: Scientists dug up a Paleolithic tooth that shows signs that these hominins may have been capable of executing a precise dental procedure.

articles.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
articles.summary: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate as police try to arrest senator
articles.summary: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.

articles.summary: Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.
